prompt:
The repository contains calc.py with a broken `median` function. Fix it so ALL of these hold: (1) it returns the median of a list of numbers, sorting a copy first (never mutating the caller's list); (2) for an even number of elements it returns the average of the two middle values as a float; (3) it raises ValueError on an empty list; (4) the existing `mean` function must keep working unchanged. Return the COMPLETE corrected contents of calc.py using EXACTLY this format (the delimiters must be on their own lines):
<<<FILE calc.py>>>
<full file content here>
<<<END>>>
Return nothing else.

inputs.name: calc.py (current, buggy)

testFiles.content:
from calc import median, mean

assert median([3, 1, 2]) == 2
assert median([1, 2, 3, 4]) == 2.5
assert median([7]) == 7
assert median([5, 2, 9, 1]) == 3.5
_src = [3, 1, 2]
_ = median(_src)
assert _src == [3, 1, 2], 'median must not mutate input'
try:
    median([])
    raise SystemExit('empty list should raise ValueError')
except ValueError:
    pass
assert mean([2, 4]) == 3
print('ok')
